refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a single performance metric: operational availability. It is expressed in a solution-neutral way, specifying what the system must achieve without implying how. The refinement clarifies the time frame ('over the agreed evaluation period') to ensure completeness and measurability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time system health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Design modular subsystems to isolate faults and enable quick repair
Establish operational procedures and safety plans to minimize downtime
Use adaptive resource allocation to maintain performance under varying conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy reduces dependency on single components, minimizing coupling
Failover mechanisms operate independently from primary components
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy as a DP supports independence by isolating failures and maintaining availability without tightly coupling subsystems.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented within cost and complexity constraints
Risk that redundancy may increase system complexity and maintenance overhead
Assumes operational policies support and do not conflict with redundancy mechanisms
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance DPs to complement redundancy and further improve availability robustness.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety including failover and redundancy mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or safety events occur

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status and sends safety-related telemetry data

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with external systems for safety coordination and failover status

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad solution module; contains many functions but failover logic would be diffused across subsystems.
Vehicle module includes hardware but failover logic spans multiple components, causing diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not failover redundancy; poor containment.
Safety Services is specifically responsible for failover and redundancy, providing high cohesion and low diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The failover capability is best contained within the Safety Services module, which is designed to handle safety-critical functions including redundancy and failover. This module provides a focused boundary minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion for the DP.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle hardware interfaces may cause some coupling.
Coordination with Teleoperation for failover in remote control scenarios may introduce interface complexity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; the selected DP aligns well with the Safety Services module boundary.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability' directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target by providing failover mechanisms to maintain system uptime. This mapping is logically sound and grounded in the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle hardware interfaces may cause some coupling.
Coordination with Teleoperation for failover in remote control scenarios may introduce interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion of the DP implementation. This focused boundary minimizes diffusion risks compared to other modules, which show higher diffusion scores and lower containment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring the Safety Services module's failover mechanisms and their effectiveness in maintaining availability above the target threshold during the evaluation period, supported by telemetry and alert interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services module is clear and well-justified, with explicit ownership and interfaces defined to support failover and redundancy functions.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; the selected DP aligns well with the Safety Services module boundary and meets the FR requirements effectively.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and alignment. The DP 'Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability' directly addresses the FR with a logically sound mapping and meets the acceptance criteria. The IM allocation to the Safety Services module shows high containment and low diffusion, minimizing architectural risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and no revisions are recommended. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition level sufficiently addresses the requirement with appropriate architectural packaging and risk mitigation, so further decomposition is not necessary at this stage.

assumptions: The Safety Services module can effectively implement and manage failover mechanisms as designed.
Telemetry and alert interfaces provide accurate monitoring of availability metrics.
Coordination with Vehicle hardware and Teleoperation interfaces will be managed to mitigate identified diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle hardware interfaces causing coupling.
Interface complexity in coordination with Teleoperation for failover scenarios.
evidence_needed: Operational data confirming availability ≥ 0.97 during failover events.
Verification reports on failover mechanism effectiveness within the Safety Services module.
